evidence_quotes.quote: some products such as video games are refrained into being services.
Those games often contain other predatory mechanics such as intrusive microtransactions in single player, full-price games.
If a player decided to buy those and the game is then shut down one day, they will lose all virtual items or cosmetics they bought.
Now, every game is being systematically killed by large corporations, including EU companies like Ubisoft, among others.
Please protect digital rights of EU citizens by enforcing video game companies to make their product or service accessible in a form that allows gaming even if it lacks certain online features.

paragraphs.text: Current online landscape is filled with anti-consumer practises at all steps of using internet. First of all, both consumers and sellers are RIGHT NOW in high danger due to payment processors such as Visa, Mastercard, or Paypal (among others). These companies de facto own all of the digital market and already decide what content can and cannot be sold effectively rendering people like me or people who want to be my consumers unable to sell or buy specific products and services that are otherwise legal. This is a gross overreach in which companies act as if they owed legal power akin to that of a government over consumers. Not only that, if a store wants to continue selling legal products banned by such companies, those companies will deny service to such store effectively rendering them unable to conduct business at all. All payment providers are united together to enforce such obstructive rules. Recently All above listed companies effectively threatened, yes threatened, companies like Valve or Itch, effectively taking down thousands of legal games for the sole reason of "Not liking such content" Be warned that those companies pretend the reason behind those rules is risk, there's no risk involved, those companies owe ENTIRE market, it won't matter if some people wrongly hate them because they allow legal products someone might not like, but it DOES impact everyone right now when those companies have de facto last say what gets to be sold online and what does not. I personally have been making a video game for a couple of months that now I cannot sell because of those rules, despite all content of my game adhering to laws of EU and around the world. This hurts not just me, but also all my clients who now not only can't buy the game, but also don't know what is going to happen to it. And that's just me, there are thousands of such sellers and tens if not houndreds of thousands of consumers hurt by those companies. Please notice that USA have an early stage act called, Fair Banking Act, and Japanese Government found Visa suspicious and forced them to undo their recent enforcements in Japan at least. Please, protect consumers and ensure every EU citizen can have a fair right to selling any product or service that is legal, regardless of individual payment processors opinion on the matter. ----- Another issue is that some products such as video games are refrained into being services. This is a huge issue for any video game consumer as companies have an ability to shut off a game, even a game that doesn't require players to have access to internet for online services, simply because they want it to be shut off so that we lose access to what should have been a product we bought and owned. Recall how all video games used too be products you could buy and own for a lifetime, even digital versions. Now, every game is being systematically killed by large corporations, including EU companies like Ubisoft, among others. Those games often contain other predatory mechanics such as intrusive microtransactions in single player, full-price games. If a player decided to buy those and the game is then shut down one day, they will lose all virtual items or cosmetics they bought. Along with stop-destroying video games initiative, EU citizens stand united against such anticonsumer approach to video games. We do not want to enforced video game companies to support games forever, instead we want companies to create products that can still be used and accessible even after servers are shut down, whether via an "End of a life patch" or by designing newer games with such mindset in mind. Please also notice that this did not used to be an issue, in fact no one but large companies use such anti-consumer practises when it comes to video games. Please protect digital rights of EU citizens by enforcing video game companies to make their product or service accessible in a form that allows gaming even if it lacks certain online features.
